title: Indulgent Peruvian Quinoa Picarones
description: Fermented, chewy quinoa picarones packed with onion and confit tomato, perfect for a post-workout ovo-vegetarian dessert.
instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water until runoff is clear.
In a saucepan, combine quinoa, water, and salt, bring to boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until quinoa is tender and water is absorbed.
Let the cooked quinoa cool to room temperature, then stir in yogurt to create a fermented batter and let rest covered at room temperature for 1 hour.
In a skillet, sauté diced onion in olive oil over medium heat until translucent, then fold in confit tomato and cook for 5 minutes.
Whisk eggs with lemon zest, then add to the quinoa batter, mixing until well combined.
Stir in whole wheat flour, baking soda, and ground cumin until the dough is thick and elastic.
Fold in the sautéed onion and tomato mixture into the dough, ensuring even distribution.
Drop spoonfuls of dough onto a parchment-lined pan, flatten slightly, and let them rest for 10 minutes to firm up.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or deep pan to 180°C and fry the picarones until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain on paper towels, sprinkle with fresh cilantro if desired, and serve warm.
